underlying: TSA daily passenger throughput, US checkpoints, 2026-04-28
method: Normal nowcast around 2.07M anchored by ongoing TSA staffing crisis from Feb-Apr 2026 government shutdown depressing late-April Tuesday throughput ~15-20% below the ~2.5M no-crisis baseline; sigma reflects volatility under disrupted operations.
notes: Could not fetch tsa.gov directly (403). 2026 has an active DHS/TSA shutdown since 2026-02-14, with 450+ TSA agents quitting, hours-long lines at major hubs, and conditions only partially improving by early April per Time/CNN/Axios coverage. Normal late-April Tuesday baseline would be ~2.45-2.55M (2025 Apr 22 ~2.49M); applied a ~15-20% shutdown haircut. Recent observations are illustrative estimates, not confirmed pulls. The T2570000 quote (mid 0.225) is almost certainly stale/wide; my model gives it ~0.0001 (floored to 0.005). Confidence is low because I lacked direct access to recent daily numbers.
